reasoning: Based on FRED statistical analysis showing 27% probability of 30-year Treasury yield exceeding 4.7% (2025-11-07 level) by 2025-11-16, with flat recent momentum. Search results indicate mixed pressures: modest Fed rate cuts expected in 2025 (0.50pp total) but long-term yields pressured by debt/deficit concerns ('higher for longer' narrative). For Dec 9 2025 and Feb 7 2026, uncertainty appropriately increases toward 0.5. The upward bias reflects the recent trend of yields rising from October lows (~4.55%) to current 4.7%, suggesting momentum toward higher yields despite expected Fed easing.
